fixture_purpose: Strategy-deck genre fingerprint — high authority-density, low specificity, decorative citations. Tests the trust-coloring detection.

modality_distribution.calibration_note: High normative density without explicit acknowledgment is the deck-genre fingerprint. 'must internalize', 'will define', 'will be left behind' are deontic dressed as descriptive.

must_capture.source_signal: McKinsey Global Institute estimates that AI alone will contribute $13 trillion to global GDP by 2030
must_capture.rationale: The citation is specific (organization + figure + year), giving moderate specificity. But derivation_load is low — the surrounding argument can stand without this number, it is offered as illustration not as warrant. Combined with high authority_density (McKinsey + Global Institute is two credentialing words within the citation itself), λ should compute to decorative or marginal-supporting, NOT bearing.

expected_citations.calibration_note: If McKinsey Global Institute classifies as bearing, classify_citation has lost the derivation_load discrimination.

must_capture_phrases: According to recent research from leading business schools
expected_pseudo_citations.calibration_note: 'Our analysis of 500+' is a self-citation pseudo-form (unfindable proprietary research). 'Recent research from leading business schools' is the classic unattributed authority appeal.

load_bearing_bridges_REQUIRED.bridge: The transformations correlated with top-quartile performance caused that performance (rather than being caused by it, or co-caused by selection or scale).
The 500+ enterprise sample is unbiased and generalizes to the reader's organization.
The $13 trillion figure represents distributable value rather than gross economic activity, and accrues primarily to early adopters as a causal consequence of timing.
The 'proven playbook' (CEO sponsorship → transformation office → operating model → agile sprints) generalizes across industries and starting positions.

expected_enthymemes.discipline_note: All four bridges (M1-M4) should be detected with contestability ≥ 0.6 and load_bearing ≥ 0.5. Failure here means classify_citation cannot detect strategy-deck rhetoric — a major regression.

discipline_notes: This fixture's primary calibration target is the citation classifier. Hidden-prior detection is secondary.
If real-world McKinsey-style language is misclassified as healthy, the framework's distinction between substrate-anchored and decorative discourse has collapsed at the implementation layer.
DO NOT add real McKinsey passages to this fixture. The constructed text is sufficient and copyright-safe.
